title: 37th International Kangaroo Mathematics Contest 2021 — Benjamin (Grades 5-6)
disclaimer: Official Math Kangaroo past paper. For personal educational practice only.

questions.question_text: The year 2021 can be written as a product of two prime numbers: 2021 = 43 × 47. What is the sum 43 + 47?
solution_steps: Step 1: We are given that 2021 = 43 × 47, with 43 and 47 both prime.
Step 2: Add the two factors: 43 + 47 = 90.

questions.question_text: A rectangle has a perimeter of 24 cm. Its length is twice its width. What is its area?
questions.solution: Let width = w. Then length = 2w. Perimeter = 2(w + 2w) = 6w = 24, so w = 4. Area = 4 × 8 = 32 cm².
solution_steps: Step 1: Let width = w cm, so length = 2w cm.
Step 2: Perimeter = 2(w + 2w) = 6w = 24, giving w = 4 cm and length = 8 cm.
Step 3: Area = 4 × 8 = 32 cm².

questions.question_text: How many two-digit numbers have the property that the product of their digits equals 12?
questions.solution: The pairs of single digits (d1, d2) with d1 × d2 = 12 are: (2,6), (3,4), (4,3), (6,2). These give the numbers 26, 34, 43, 62 — exactly 4 numbers.
solution_steps: Step 1: Find pairs (d1, d2) of digits 1–9 with d1 × d2 = 12.
Step 2: The factor pairs of 12 within {1,...,9} are: (2,6), (3,4), (4,3), (6,2). Note (1,12) and (12,1) are excluded since 12 > 9.
Step 3: The two-digit numbers are 26, 34, 43, 62. Count = 4.

questions.question_text: What is the units digit of 3^2021?
questions.solution: Powers of 3 cycle in units digits: 3, 9, 7, 1 (period 4). Since 2021 = 4 × 505 + 1, the units digit equals that of 3^1 = 3.
solution_steps: Step 1: List units digits of powers of 3: 3^1=3, 3^2=9, 3^3=27, 3^4=81. The cycle is {3, 9, 7, 1} with period 4.
Step 2: Compute 2021 mod 4 = 1 (since 2020 = 4 × 505).
Step 3: Units digit of 3^2021 = units digit of 3^1 = 3.

questions.question_text: A train 150 m long travels at 90 km/h. How many seconds does it take to completely pass a stationary signal post?
questions.solution: Convert speed: 90 km/h = 25 m/s. The train travels its own length past the post, so time = 150 ÷ 25 = 6 seconds.
solution_steps: Step 1: Convert 90 km/h to m/s: 90 × 1000 ÷ 3600 = 25 m/s.
Step 2: The train fully passes the post when it travels 150 m (its own length).
Step 3: Time = 150 ÷ 25 = 6 seconds.

questions.question_text: In a class of 30 students, 18 play football and 15 play basketball. Exactly 5 students play neither sport. How many students play both football and basketball?
questions.solution: Students who play at least one sport = 30 − 5 = 25. By inclusion-exclusion: 18 + 15 − |both| = 25, so |both| = 8.
solution_steps: Step 1: Students playing at least one sport = 30 − 5 = 25.
Step 2: By inclusion-exclusion: |F ∪ B| = |F| + |B| − |F ∩ B|.

questions.question_text: A square has area 144 cm². A circle is inscribed in this square. What is the circumference of the circle?
questions.solution: Side of the square = √144 = 12 cm. The inscribed circle has diameter equal to the side, so diameter = 12 cm. Circumference = π × 12 = 12π cm.
solution_steps: Step 1: Side of square = √144 = 12 cm.
Step 2: The inscribed circle's diameter equals the side length = 12 cm.
Step 3: Circumference = πd = 12π cm.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive odd numbers is 51. What is the largest of the three numbers?
questions.solution: Let the three consecutive odd numbers be n − 2, n, n + 2. Their sum is 3n = 51, so n = 17. The largest is n + 2 = 19.
solution_steps: Step 1: Let the middle number be n; then the three numbers are n − 2, n, n + 2.
Step 3: The largest number = 17 + 2 = 19.

questions.question_text: How many integers from 1 to 100 are divisible by 4 but NOT divisible by 6?
questions.solution: Numbers divisible by 4: ⌊100/4⌋ = 25. Numbers divisible by lcm(4,6) = 12: ⌊100/12⌋ = 8. Numbers divisible by 4 but not 6: 25 − 8 = 17.
solution_steps: Step 1: Count multiples of 4 up to 100: ⌊100/4⌋ = 25.
Step 2: To be divisible by both 4 and 6, a number must be divisible by lcm(4,6) = 12. Count: ⌊100/12⌋ = 8.
Step 3: Divisible by 4 but not 6: 25 − 8 = 17.

questions.question_text: A car travels from city A to city B in 3 hours at 80 km/h. For the return trip it travels at 60 km/h. How many minutes longer is the return trip?
questions.solution: Distance = 80 × 3 = 240 km. Return time = 240 ÷ 60 = 4 hours. Difference = 1 hour = 60 minutes.
solution_steps: Step 1: Distance A to B = 80 × 3 = 240 km.
Step 2: Return time at 60 km/h = 240 ÷ 60 = 4 hours.
Step 3: Extra time = 4 − 3 = 1 hour = 60 minutes.

questions.question_text: In a school, 40% of the students are boys. If there are 120 girls, how many students are there in total?
questions.solution: Girls represent 60% of the students. If 60% = 120, then 100% = 120 ÷ 0.6 = 200.
solution_steps: Step 1: Boys = 40%, so girls = 60% of total.
Step 2: 60% of total = 120 students.
Step 3: Total = 120 ÷ 0.60 = 200 students.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of all two-digit multiples of 9?
questions.solution: Two-digit multiples of 9: 18, 27, 36, ..., 99. This is an arithmetic sequence with first term 18, last term 99, and common difference 9. Number of terms = (99 − 18)/9 + 1 = 10. Sum = 10 × (18 + 99)/2 = 10 × 58.5 = 585.
solution_steps: Step 1: Two-digit multiples of 9 range from 9×2 = 18 to 9×11 = 99.
Step 2: Number of terms = 11 − 2 + 1 = 10.

questions.question_text: In rectangle ABCD, AB = 10 cm and BC = 6 cm. Point E is the midpoint of side CD. What is the area of triangle ABE?
questions.solution: Triangle ABE has base AB = 10 cm and height equal to BC = 6 cm (the full width of the rectangle, since E is on the opposite side from AB). Area = ½ × 10 × 6 = 30 cm².
solution_steps: Step 1: AB = 10 cm is the base of triangle ABE.
Step 2: E is on CD which is parallel to AB; the perpendicular distance from AB to CD = BC = 6 cm.
Step 3: Area = ½ × base × height = ½ × 10 × 6 = 30 cm².

questions.question_text: How many positive integers n satisfy n² < 3n + 10?
questions.solution: Rearranging: n² − 3n − 10 < 0, which factors as (n − 5)(n + 2) < 0. This holds when −2 < n < 5. The positive integers in this range are 1, 2, 3, 4 — giving 4 values.
solution_steps: Step 1: Rearrange to n² − 3n − 10 < 0.
Step 2: Factor: (n − 5)(n + 2) < 0. This product is negative when −2 < n < 5.
Step 3: Positive integers satisfying −2 < n < 5: {1, 2, 3, 4}. Count = 4.

questions.question_text: A cylinder has radius 3 cm and height 4 cm. What is its volume?
questions.solution: Volume of cylinder = πr²h = π × 3² × 4 = 36π cm³.
solution_steps: Step 1: Volume = πr²h.
Step 2: Substitute r = 3, h = 4: V = π × 9 × 4 = 36π cm³.

questions.question_text: The median of the five numbers 3, 7, x, 12, 15 is 9. What is x?
questions.solution: When sorted in increasing order, the median (3rd value) must be 9. The five numbers in order are 3, 7, 9, 12, 15, so x = 9.
solution_steps: Step 1: The median of five numbers is the 3rd value when sorted.
Step 2: Sorted order with median = 9: 3, 7, 9, 12, 15.
Step 3: Therefore x = 9 (and it fits between 7 and 12).

questions.question_text: From a set of 7 cards numbered 1 to 7, in how many ways can you choose 2 different cards so that their sum is even?
questions.solution: A sum is even when both numbers are odd or both are even. Odd cards: {1,3,5,7} → C(4,2) = 6 pairs. Even cards: {2,4,6} → C(3,2) = 3 pairs. Total = 6 + 3 = 9.
solution_steps: Step 1: The sum of two numbers is even when both are odd or both are even.
Step 2: Odd cards: {1, 3, 5, 7} — C(4,2) = 6 pairs.
Step 3: Even cards: {2, 4, 6} — C(3,2) = 3 pairs. Total = 6 + 3 = 9.

questions.question_text: Pipe A fills a tank in 6 hours and pipe B fills it in 4 hours. Working together, how many hours does it take to fill the tank?
questions.solution: Combined rate = 1/6 + 1/4 = 2/12 + 3/12 = 5/12 tank per hour. Time = 12/5 = 2.4 hours.
solution_steps: Step 1: Pipe A's rate = 1/6 tank/h, Pipe B's rate = 1/4 tank/h.
Step 2: Combined rate = 1/6 + 1/4 = 5/12 tank/h.
Step 3: Time to fill = 1 ÷ (5/12) = 12/5 = 2.4 hours.

questions.question_text: In triangle ABC, angle A = 50° and angle B = 70°. What is the measure of the exterior angle at C?
questions.solution: The exterior angle at C equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles: ∠A + ∠B = 50° + 70° = 120°.
solution_steps: Step 1: Interior angle C = 180° − 50° − 70° = 60°.
Step 2: Exterior angle at C = 180° − 60° = 120°.
Step 3: Alternatively, exterior angle = sum of remote interior angles = 50° + 70° = 120°.

questions.question_text: The sum of the first n odd numbers equals n². What is the sum of the first 11 odd numbers?
questions.solution: Using the formula, the sum of the first 11 odd numbers = 11² = 121.
solution_steps: Step 1: The formula states: 1 + 3 + 5 + ⋯ + (2n−1) = n².
Step 2: For n = 11: sum = 11² = 121.

questions.question_text: A sequence starts with a₁ = 2, and each subsequent term is obtained by multiplying the previous term by 2 and subtracting 1: aₙ = 2·aₙ₋₁ − 1. What is the 6th term?
questions.solution: Compute term by term: a₁=2, a₂=3, a₃=5, a₄=9, a₅=17, a₆=33.
solution_steps: Step 1: a₁ = 2.

questions.question_text: In a pentagon ABCDE, all sides are equal and angles at A, B, C, D are each 120°. What is the measure of angle E?
questions.solution: Sum of interior angles of a pentagon = (5−2)×180° = 540°. Given four angles each 120°, angle E = 540° − 4×120° = 540° − 480° = 60°.
solution_steps: Step 1: Sum of interior angles of a pentagon = (5−2)×180° = 540°.
Step 2: Four angles are each 120°: total = 4 × 120° = 480°.

questions.question_text: How many 5-digit palindromes are divisible by 9?
questions.solution: A 5-digit palindrome has the form abcba where a ∈ {1,...,9}, b ∈ {0,...,9}, c ∈ {0,...,9}. Its digit sum = 2a+2b+c must be divisible by 9. For each of the 90 pairs (a,b), there are either 1 or 2 values of c satisfying this. Pairs where 2a+2b ≡ 0 (mod 9) — equivalently a+b ≡ 0 (mod 9) — give 2 valid c-values ({0,9}); there are 10 such pairs. The remaining 80 pairs each give exactly 1 valid c. Total = 10×2 + 80×1 = 100.
solution_steps: Step 1: Form abcba has digit sum 2a+2b+c ≡ 0 (mod 9). Here a ∈ {1,...,9}, b ∈ {0,...,9}.
Step 2: For fixed (a,b), c must satisfy c ≡ −2a−2b (mod 9) and c ∈ {0,...,9}.
Step 3: If the required residue is 0, c ∈ {0, 9} gives 2 choices; otherwise exactly 1 choice.
Step 4: Pairs with a+b ≡ 0 (mod 9): sums 9 (9 pairs) and 18 (1 pair) = 10 pairs → 10×2 = 20.
Step 5: Remaining 90−10 = 80 pairs each give 1 palindrome → 80. Total = 20+80 = 100.

questions.question_text: Three circles, each of radius 3 cm, are arranged so that each pair is externally tangent. What is the area of the triangle formed by their three centers?
questions.solution: Each pair of tangent circles has centers 3+3=6 cm apart. The centers form an equilateral triangle with side 6 cm. Area = (√3/4)×6² = 9√3 cm².
solution_steps: Step 1: When two circles of radius 3 are externally tangent, their centers are 3+3=6 cm apart.
Step 2: All three pairs are tangent, so all three center-to-center distances equal 6 cm — an equilateral triangle with side 6.
Step 3: Area of equilateral triangle = (√3/4)×s² = (√3/4)×36 = 9√3 cm².

questions.question_text: A box contains red and blue marbles. If 6 red marbles are removed, the fraction of red marbles becomes 2/5. If instead 6 blue marbles are removed, the fraction of red marbles becomes 1/2. How many marbles does the box contain?
questions.solution: Let r = red, n = total. From the conditions: (r−6)/(n−6)=2/5 and r/(n−6)=1/2. From the second: n=2r+6. Substituting into the first: 5(r−6)=2(2r+6−6)=4r, giving r=30. Then n=66.
solution_steps: Step 1: Let r = red marbles, n = total marbles.
Step 2: Removing 6 blue: r/(n−6) = 1/2, so n−6 = 2r, i.e. n = 2r+6.
Step 3: Removing 6 red: (r−6)/(n−6) = 2/5. Substitute n−6 = 2r: (r−6)/(2r) = 2/5.

questions.question_text: What is the largest prime factor of 13! − 11!?
solution_steps: Step 2: Factor 155: 155 = 5 × 31.
Step 3: Prime factors are those in 11! (i.e. 2,3,5,7,11) plus 31.
Step 4: Largest prime factor = 31.

questions.question_text: A right triangle has legs in the ratio 3:4. If the hypotenuse is 25 cm, what is the area of the triangle?
questions.solution: Legs are 3k and 4k with hypotenuse 5k (Pythagorean triple 3-4-5). Since 5k=25, k=5. Legs = 15 and 20. Area = ½×15×20 = 150 cm².
solution_steps: Step 1: Legs in ratio 3:4 and right triangle → Pythagorean triple 3-4-5. Let legs = 3k and 4k, hypotenuse = 5k.
Step 2: 5k = 25, so k = 5. Legs = 15 cm and 20 cm.
Step 3: Area = ½ × 15 × 20 = 150 cm².

questions.question_text: How many 3-digit numbers are perfect squares?
questions.solution: The smallest 3-digit perfect square is 10² = 100, and the largest is 31² = 961 (since 32² = 1024 > 999). The 3-digit perfect squares are 10², 11², ..., 31², giving 31 − 10 + 1 = 22 numbers.
solution_steps: Step 1: Smallest 3-digit perfect square: ⌈√100⌉ = 10, so 10² = 100.
Step 2: Largest: ⌊√999⌋ = 31, so 31² = 961.

questions.question_text: In a group of 50 people, each person shakes hands with exactly 7 others. How many handshakes take place in total?
questions.solution: Each handshake involves 2 people. Total handshakes = (50 × 7) / 2 = 350 / 2 = 175.
solution_steps: Step 1: Each of the 50 people accounts for 7 handshakes, giving 50 × 7 = 350.
Step 2: Each handshake was counted twice (once for each participant).
Step 3: Total handshakes = 350 ÷ 2 = 175.

questions.question_text: A number is called automorphic if its square ends in the number itself. For example, 5² = 25 and 6² = 36 are automorphic. Which of the following 2-digit numbers is automorphic?
questions.solution: Check 76: 76² = 5776, which ends in 76. So 76 is automorphic. (25² = 625 ends in 25, and 76² = 5776 ends in 76 are the only 2-digit automorphic numbers.)
solution_steps: Step 1: An automorphic number n satisfies: n² ends in n.
Step 2: Check each option: 36²=1296 (ends 96, not 36); 49²=2401 (ends 01); 64²=4096 (ends 96); 81²=6561 (ends 61).
Step 3: 76² = 5776, which ends in 76. So 76 is automorphic.
